findings.title: Skills have no handler for reviewer BLOCK/REVISE verdicts
findings.description: Neither skills/plan/SKILL.md nor skills/implement/SKILL.md contain any conditional logic for reviewer BLOCK or REVISE verdicts — they dispatch reviewers then proceed to the gate step regardless, leaving the orchestrating LLM to infer the correct response with no explicit instruction.

findings.title: LEAN-mode reviewer skip removes the only guard against research-injected handoff content
findings.description: reviewer-safety checks for [INJECTION-WARNING] markers from researcher output, but in LEAN mode the lean-risk-classify.mjs can skip all reviewers — meaning web-sourced research injection flows through coder handoff to implementer with no safety check.

findings.title: reviewer-boundary naming split across triage excerpts, output paths, and dispatch tables
findings.description: Agent is named reviewer-boundary in agents/reviewer-boundary.md but triage uses bare 'reviewer' for excerpt paths (triage-excerpts/reviewer.md), output paths (reviewer-output/reviewer.md), and dispatch table column headers, while JSON examples use 'reviewer-boundary' — a rename on either side breaks silently.

findings.title: lean-risk-classify.mjs missing coverage for signal-format and .pipeline/ schema changes
findings.description: CLAUDE.md defines 'signal formats' and 'persisted .pipeline/* shape' as risk-surface triggers, but scripts/lean-risk-classify.mjs has no regex for signal format changes or .pipeline/ JSON schema mutations — such handoffs pass the LEAN gate and skip reviewers.

findings.title: No skill orchestrates the coder revision loop after reviewer REVISE
findings.description: The coder agent has a [revision-mode: N] protocol for re-drafting after REVISE verdicts, but no skill contains a step that re-invokes the coder with this prefix — the revision capability exists in the agent but is unreachable from the pipeline.

findings.title: Model routing block duplicated verbatim across 5 pipeline skills
findings.description: The identical 8-line 'Model routing' protocol block is copy-pasted into skills/plan/SKILL.md, skills/implement/SKILL.md, skills/apply/SKILL.md, skills/debug/SKILL.md, and skills/refactor/SKILL.md — any routing protocol change requires 5 parallel edits.

findings.title: Approve and discard skills give generic confirmation with no feature name or context
findings.description: skills/approve/SKILL.md prints 'Gate 1 approved' without the feature name, and skills/discard/SKILL.md prints 'Gate discarded' without gate number or feature — the user must remember what was pending.

findings.title: TRIVIAL and SPRINT modes are undocumented in any skill's mode-decision logic
findings.description: CLAUDE.md defines TRIVIAL and SPRINT pipeline modes and hooks enforce them, but no skill (plan, implement, apply) contains decision logic to select or operate in these modes — they only reference LEAN, STANDARD, and FULL.

findings.title: 6+ agent files in agents/ are unreferenced by any active skill
findings.description: integrity-checker, tool-call-auditor, regression-risk, agent-optimizer, skills-generator, and researcher-triage exist in agents/ but are never dispatched by any skill SKILL.md — they appear only in templates and worktrees, suggesting they are dead or deprecated agents.

findings.title: Discard deletes gate file while approve updates it — asymmetric lifecycle
findings.description: skills/discard/SKILL.md deletes gate-pending.json on discard, while skills/approve/SKILL.md updates it in-place on approve — any downstream code expecting the gate file to exist after all terminal states will fail on discard.
